remedy: Apply copper-based fungicides. Prune and remove infected lower leaves to prevent soil splash.
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Plant resistant varieties next season and rotate crops.
Apply chlorothalonil immediately. Destroy severely infected plants. Ensure high airflow.

symptoms: Small circular spots with gray centers
remedy: Apply fungicidal sprays early in the season. Mulch around the base to prevent soil splashing.

symptoms: Black, sunken leathery spots on bottom of fruit
remedy: Maintain consistent soil moisture. Apply a calcium supplement to the soil or as a foliar spray.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: No cure. Uproot and destroy infected plants. Wash hands and tools thoroughly; avoid smoking near plants.
Avoid overhead watering. Apply a fixed copper fungicide mixed with mancozeb.

symptoms: Sunken, water-soaked spots on ripe fruit
Pinkish spore masses in center of spots
remedy: Harvest fruit as soon as it ripens. Apply preventative fungicides and rotate crops.
Solarize soil in summer. Plant marigolds as a deterrent. Use nematode-resistant tomato varieties.

symptoms: Fine webbing on undersides of leaves
remedy: Spray plants with a strong stream of water. Apply insecticidal soap or neem oil every 7 days.
Apply chlorothalonil fungicide. Ensure proper plant spacing for airflow. Do not compost infected tubers.
Lower soil pH to between 5.0 and 5.2. Avoid using fresh manure, and ensure consistent watering.

symptoms: Hard black raised masses on tubers
remedy: Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes. Delay planting until soil warms up to speed emergence.
Handpick beetles and larvae. Apply Spinosad or Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) targeted at young larvae.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: No cure. Remove infected plants to prevent aphid transmission. Use certified seed potatoes.

symptoms: Black, slimy rotting at the stem base
remedy: Improve soil drainage. Avoid planting in cold, wet soils. Remove and destroy infected plants.

symptoms: Wrinkled, sunken areas on tubers in storage
White or pink mold on tuber surface
remedy: Handle tubers carefully during harvest to prevent bruising. Cure potatoes properly before long-term storage.
Harvest promptly after vines die. Maintain proper humidity and cool temperatures in storage.
Rotate crops away from grasses. Cultivate soil thoroughly before planting to expose larvae to birds.
Apply preventative fungicides. Keep plants healthy with proper nutrition to resist infection.

symptoms: Spots turn brown and leave holes
remedy: Use copper-based bactericides. Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops away from nightshades.

symptoms: Dark brown lesions on stem base
White fungal growth on rotting fruit
remedy: Improve soil drainage aggressively. Plant on raised beds. Remove affected plants immediately.

symptoms: Black, sunken leathery spots on bottom of fruit
remedy: Ensure even watering. Apply calcium spray to foliage or amend soil with lime before planting.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: Control aphid populations which spread the virus. Remove weeds. Destroy infected plants.

symptoms: Pale, papery patches on fruit exposed to sun
remedy: Maintain a healthy foliage canopy to shade the fruit. Avoid heavy pruning. Provide artificial shade if necessary.

symptoms: Large circular spots with light gray centers
remedy: Apply copper fungicides. Remove plant debris at the end of the season. Ensure good spacing.
Apply sulfur or insecticidal soap. Often requires magnification to see pests; treat preemptively if symptoms appear.

symptoms: White fungal threads at soil line
Small, mustard-seed-like structures at stem base
remedy: Deep plow soil to bury sclerotia. Remove infected plants and surrounding soil. Avoid excessive organic matter on the surface.
Plant nematode-resistant varieties. Use crop rotation with non-hosts like corn.

symptoms: Sunken, water-soaked spots on ripe fruit
Pinkish spore masses in center of spots
remedy: Use disease-free seed. Apply preventative fungicides during warm, wet weather.
Apply neem oil, sulfur, or potassium bicarbonate sprays. Ensure good air circulation.

symptoms: Purple-gray fuzz on undersides of leaves
remedy: Apply specific downy mildew fungicides. Avoid overhead watering. Plant resistant varieties.

symptoms: Sticky, stringy sap when stem is cut
remedy: Control cucumber beetles early in the season with row covers or insecticides. Destroy infected plants.

symptoms: Spots dry and fall out leaving holes
White exudate on spots during high humidity
remedy: Use copper-based bactericides. Avoid working in the garden when plants are wet to prevent spread.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: Control aphids. Remove infected plants immediately. Use resistant varieties.
Apply chlorothalonil. Rotate crops. Do not save seed from infected plants.

symptoms: Small circular spots with light brown centers
remedy: Improve ventilation in greenhouses. Apply appropriate fungicides. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Handpick bugs and crush eggs. Apply neem oil to nymphs. Trap adults under boards overnight and destroy.
Spray with strong water blast. Introduce ladybugs. Use insecticidal soap.

symptoms: Yellowing leaves, often starting on one side
remedy: Use resistant varieties. Maintain soil pH between 6.5 and 7.0. Avoid excessive nitrogen.
Apply neem oil or sulfur spray. Plant resistant varieties. Prune overlapping leaves to increase airflow.

symptoms: Sawdust-like frass near base of stem
remedy: Slit stem vertically to find and kill the white grub. Bury damaged stem under soil to encourage new roots. Use row covers early.

symptoms: Black, sunken leathery spots on bottom of fruit
remedy: Maintain consistent watering. Mulch soil to retain moisture. Test soil for calcium deficiency.

symptoms: Purple-gray fuzz on undersides of leaves
remedy: Apply targeted fungicides. Water at the base of the plant only. Destroy infected debris.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: Manage aphid vectors. Destroy infected plants immediately. Keep weed hosts clear from garden borders.

symptoms: Soft, wet rot on blossoms and young fruit
Fuzzy fungal growth resembling black pins
remedy: Avoid overhead watering. Space plants well. Remove aborted or rotting fruit immediately.
Control leafhopper insects which spread the phytoplasma. Remove and destroy infected plants.
Use resistant varieties. Apply preventative fungicides during cool, moist weather.

symptoms: Soft rot at the crown/stem base
remedy: Rotate crops for at least 3 years. Ensure excellent soil drainage. Treat seed with fungicide before planting.

symptoms: Fine webbing on undersides of leaves
remedy: Apply horticultural oils or insecticidal soap. Keep plants well-watered, as mites thrive on drought-stressed plants.
Rake up and destroy fallen leaves in autumn. Apply preventative fungicide sprays in early spring.
Prune out infected branches 8 inches below visible damage during dry weather. Sterilize tools between cuts. Apply copper sprays during dormancy.

symptoms: Orange, gelatinous horns on nearby juniper trees
remedy: Remove nearby eastern red cedar/juniper hosts. Apply preventative fungicides starting at blossom stage.
Prune canopy to increase airflow. Apply sulfur-based fungicides or myclobutanil early in the season.

symptoms: Dark brown lesions on fruit with concentric rings
Frog-eye pattern on leaves (purple margin with light center)
remedy: Remove dead wood and mummified fruit during winter. Apply Captan or other protective fungicides.

symptoms: Smudgy black patches on fruit surface
Tiny black specks grouped on fruit skin
remedy: Prune for better air circulation and sunlight penetration. Apply fungicides during late summer humid periods.

symptoms: Sawdust-like frass near base of stem or fruit core
remedy: Hang pheromone traps. Bag young fruit. Apply targeted insecticides timed with moth hatching.

symptoms: Large, woody, tumor-like growths at soil line
remedy: No cure once infected. Inspect nursery stock carefully before planting. Avoid injuring bark near the soil.
Apply dormant oil in late winter to smother eggs. Use insecticidal soap during the growing season.

symptoms: Sunken, dark spots on the fruit surface
Brown, corky tissue under the fruit skin
remedy: Maintain consistent soil moisture. Apply foliar calcium sprays during fruit development. Avoid excessive nitrogen.

symptoms: Raised, corky lesions with yellow halos on leaves
remedy: Highly contagious. Use copper sprays to protect new flush. Destroy severely infected trees if mandated by local agriculture boards.

symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: No cure. Control the Asian Citrus Psyllid vector with insecticides. Apply specialized nutrient foliar sprays to prolong tree life. Remove declining trees.

symptoms: Spots turn dark and greasy looking
remedy: Apply copper fungicides or horticultural oils in mid-summer to protect new leaf flushes.

symptoms: Tiny, raised reddish-brown pustules on leaves and fruit
remedy: Prune out dead wood where the fungus survives. Apply copper fungicide 2-3 weeks after petal fall.

symptoms: Black, powdery mold coating leaves and fruit
Presence of aphids, whiteflies, or scale
remedy: The mold is harmless but blocks sunlight. Treat the underlying insect infestation (aphids/scale) with horticultural oil or soap. The mold will wash away over time.

symptoms: Bark peeling and gumming at the soil line
remedy: Keep soil pulled away from the graft union. Improve drainage. Apply systemic fungicides like Aliette or Ridomil.

symptoms: Quick decline and death of the tree
Pitting on wood under the bark
remedy: Use virus-free budwood grafted onto resistant rootstocks (like trifoliate orange). Control aphids.

symptoms: Raised, warty lesions on fruit and leaves
remedy: Apply preventative copper or ferbam sprays during early spring flush and again at petal fall.

symptoms: Silvery, winding trails mined into leaves
remedy: Apply Spinosad or horticultural oils. Mature trees tolerate damage well, but young trees need protection.

symptoms: Light brown, leathery rot on fruit, primarily lower canopy
remedy: Prune tree skirts 24 inches above the ground. Apply copper sprays to the lower canopy before heavy winter rains.
Avoid overhead watering to keep foliage dry. Spray with neem oil or a systemic fungicide.
Improve air circulation. Apply sulfur-based sprays or potassium bicarbonate.
Fatal virus spread by microscopic mites. Immediately dig up and bag the entire plant, including roots. Do not compost.

symptoms: Purple or red angular spots on leaves
remedy: Requires specific fungicides (not the same as powdery mildew). Ensure excellent airflow and dry foliage.

symptoms: Orange, powdery pustules on undersides of leaves
Yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces
remedy: Remove infected leaves. Apply preventative fungicides containing myclobutanil or tebuconazole.
Remove spent blooms promptly. Avoid wetting flowers. Space plants for airflow.

symptoms: Wavy yellow lines or rings on leaves
Mottled light and dark green leaves
remedy: No cure. Usually not fatal but reduces aesthetics and cold hardiness. Replace plant if decline is severe. Buy certified virus-free stock.
Wash aphids off with a strong blast of water. Apply insecticidal soap or introduce ladybugs.

symptoms: Metallic green and copper beetles present
remedy: Handpick beetles into soapy water in early morning. Use row covers. Avoid pheromone traps as they attract more beetles.

symptoms: Fine webbing on undersides of leaves
remedy: Spray plants aggressively with water to dislodge. Apply horticultural oil if infestation is severe.

symptoms: Light brown spots with dark borders on leaves
Grapes turn into hard, black mummies
remedy: Remove all mummified fruit during winter. Apply Captan or myclobutanil from bud break until grapes begin to color.
Prune canopy for sunlight penetration. Apply sulfur or specialized fungicides routinely through the season.

symptoms: Yellowish oily spots on upper leaf surface
White, cottony growth on leaf undersides
remedy: Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure excellent drainage and airflow. Remove infected fallen leaves.

symptoms: Fuzzy gray mold on ripening grapes
remedy: Open up the fruiting zone by removing leaves around clusters. Apply specific botryticides during bloom, bunch closure, and veraison.

symptoms: Leaf margins turn yellow or red, then dry up
Matchstick petioles (leaves drop but stems stay)
remedy: No cure. Fatal bacterial disease spread by sharpshooter insects. Remove infected vines. Control weed hosts nearby.

symptoms: Tiny dark spots with yellow halos on leaves
remedy: Prune out infected canes during winter. Apply protective fungicides starting at early shoot growth.

symptoms: Premature ripening and splitting of fruit
remedy: Use pheromone disruption ties. Spray targeted insecticides (like Bt) just as eggs hatch.

symptoms: Large, woody, tumor-like growths on trunk or vines
remedy: Protect trunks from freeze injury, which allows bacteria to enter. Prune below galls and train a new trunk if possible, or replace vine.

symptoms: Leaves turn red or yellow in late summer but veins stay green
remedy: No cure. Spread by mealybugs. Plant certified virus-free vines. Remove infected vines if establishing a new vineyard.

symptoms: Blister-like swellings on upper leaf surface
White, felt-like fuzz on undersides of blisters
remedy: Damage is mostly cosmetic and rarely requires chemical treatment. Sulfur sprays for powdery mildew usually suppress mites.

symptoms: Fuzzy gray mold on ripening fruit
remedy: Space plants for airflow. Grow on plastic mulch or straw to keep fruit off wet soil. Apply fungicides during bloom.
Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate. Plant in full sun. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer.

symptoms: Roots have a reddish-brown core when sliced open
remedy: Improve soil drainage dramatically. Plant on raised beds. Use resistant varieties. Do not replant in infected soil.

symptoms: Small spots with light gray centers and dark purple borders
remedy: Remove severely infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicides. Renew strawberry beds every few years.

symptoms: Dull, tough, leathery texture on fruit
remedy: Prevent soil from splashing onto fruit using mulch. Ensure good drainage. Apply fosetyl-al or mefenoxam.

symptoms: Sunken, dark circular spots on fruit
Pinkish spore masses in center of spots
remedy: Use disease-free transplants. Apply captan or pyraclostrobin. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Do not plant strawberries where tomatoes, potatoes, or peppers were recently grown. Use resistant varieties.
Use iron phosphate bait pellets. Handpick at night. Trap with shallow saucers of beer.

symptoms: Seeds crowded at the tip of the berry
remedy: Keep weeds mowed around the berry patch. Apply insecticides or insecticidal soap before blooms open.

symptoms: Fine webbing on undersides of leaves
remedy: Release predatory mites. Apply horticultural oils. Maintain consistent watering.
